1. Library Overview, Physical Details and Stack Collection Statistics
Chunk ID: msajce_library_chunk_01 | URL: https://www.msajce-edu.in/library.php | ~490 tokens
Introduction to the MSAJCE Learning Centre (Library) covering area, technology, collection size, and complete stack collection statistics.
Welcome to the Learning Centre  MSAJCE
Library is the Heart of every academic Institution. Our institution encourages all students and staff members to make the best
use of the library that has been carefully built up since the inception of the college as a cardinal support service of the Institute.
The prime objective of our library is to provide the right information to the right readers at the right time in the right manner by
bibliographic sources and full text sources in the form of print as well as digital in the disciplines of Science, Engineering,
Technology, Humanities, Management, General and etc.
The collections are kept in separate places such as the stack section, reference section, and Periodical Section to support the
scholarly and informational needs of the Institute Community. The library is housed in a spacious area of 8978 Sq.ft (Ground
Floor and 1st Floor), fully computerized with more than 29853 books and 1885 reference volumes stacked for the benefit of staff
and students. The library subscribes around 35 nos specialized Printed journals and 20 nos magazines. We are also a member
of DELNET. J-Gate database is subscribed yearly.
Technology: Bar-coded Technology, open source software called Koha, internet connectivity and WIFI facility are all available.
Library Resources  Stack Collection Details:

2. Library Working Hours and Printed Journals by Department
Chunk ID: msajce_library_chunk_02 | URL: https://www.msajce-edu.in/library.php | ~390 tokens
Library opening hours on weekdays and Sundays, and the department-wise distribution of printed journal subscriptions totalling 37
journals.
Library Working Hours:
Monday to Saturday  8.00 A.M. to 7.00 P.M.
All Sundays  10.00 A.M. to 4.00 P.M.
List of Printed Journals  Department-wise:
Note: The library subscribes 35 printed journals mentioned in the overview; the department-wise total shows 37, reflecting the
full subscribed count across all departments. CSE/IT has the highest number of printed journal subscriptions (10), followed by
Mechanical, ECE, and EEE (6 each), S&H; (5), and Civil Engineering (4).
Department-wise Printed Journal Subscriptions

6. Institution Membership and Library Rules and Regulations
Chunk ID: msajce_library_chunk_06 | URL: https://www.msajce-edu.in/library.php | ~510 tokens
MSAJCE library institutional memberships (DELNET and J-Gate), and the complete set of library rules, regulations, and fine structure for
overdue books.
Institution Membership:
1. Developing Library Network (DELNET)  International Library Network: Avail any book, Xerox or soft copy of any journal
article through ILL (Inter Library Loan). URL: https://delnet.in/
2. J-Gate (Database): More than 50,684 journals are available in the online website. URL: https://jgateplus.com/
Library Rules and Regulations:
(cid:127) All Staff and Students are the members of our College Library.
(cid:127) All students are eligible to borrow 18 Books.
(cid:127) All staff are eligible to borrow Books.
(cid:127) Reference books and periodicals will NOT be issued.
(cid:127) Library ID is not transferable.
(cid:127) Member should notify the Librarian any loss of cards and change of address immediately.
(cid:127) The User may reserve a book in circulation. He will be notified as soon as the reserved book is returned. The book will be
retained at the circulation desk for one day from the date of return.
(cid:127) Books will be issued for a short duration of 30 days only.
Fine Structure for Overdue Books:
(cid:127) After 30 days  First 7 days (days 31 37): Rs. 1.00 per day per book
(cid:127) After 30 days  8 to 14 days (days 38 44): Rs. 2.00 per day per book
(cid:127) After 30 days  15 days onwards (day 45+): Rs. 5.00 per day per book
Additional Rules:
(cid:127) Silence should be observed in the Library.
(cid:127) Users should leave personal belongings (bags, briefcase, notebooks, files) at the entrance.
(cid:127) Loss of books must be reported immediately to the Librarian. If the borrower is unable to replace the lost book, the cost (double
amount) will be recovered from the borrower.
(cid:127) All books must be returned to the Library in time for physical stock verification.
(cid:127) Students are advised to check whether the book contains all pages intact before borrowing. At the time of returning, if any
damage or defect is found, the borrower will be held responsible.
Library Fine Structure for Overdue Books
Overdue Period Fine Rate
Days 31 37 (First 7 days after 30-day loan period) Rs. 1.00 per day per book
Days 38 44 (8 to 14 days after 30-day loan period) Rs. 2.00 per day per book
Day 45 onwards (15 days onwards after 30-day loan period) Rs. 5.00 per day per book
